name: Is it safe to ask AI about my legal case or will it be used against me in court?
description: No, AI chats aren't privileged and can be court evidence, per 2026 NY ruling—delete them and talk to a real lawyer instead.[1][3]

mainEntity.name: Is it safe to ask AI about my legal case or will it be used against me in court?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, AI chats aren't privileged and can be court evidence, per 2026 NY ruling—delete them and talk to a real lawyer instead.[1][3]
acceptedAnswer.description:
No, it's not safe—AI conversations are not privileged and can be discoverable as evidence.[1][2][3] In the 2026 *US v. Heppner* federal court ruling in New York, a defendant's AI-generated legal memos were ruled non-privileged because they weren't created under attorney direction and lacked confidentiality.[1][3] This applies nationwide in civil and criminal cases, as device seizures or discovery can reveal prompts, inconsistencies, or speculations that harm your position.[1][2]

**Key factors:**
- No attorney-client privilege with AI; it's a third party.[3]
- Not work product unless lawyer directs use.[1]
- Stored data (browser history, logs) often recoverable.[1]
- Inconsistencies between AI chats and testimony weaponized.[1]

Delete records immediately, avoid AI for strategy, and consult a lawyer before discussing cases online. Use only lawyer-directed AI tools for potential protection.[1] Seek qualified counsel experienced in AI evidence issues.
